draft.introduction: Ruth 1 narrates Naomi’s return from Moab and Ruth’s famous declaration of loyalty: “Where you go I will go.” The story of a Moabite woman entering Israel’s covenant community challenges ethnic exclusivism.

translation.text: But Ruth said, ‘Do not urge me to leave you or to return from following you. For where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people, and your God my God. Where you die I will die, and there will I be buried.’
translation.original_text: εἶπεν δὲ Ρουθ μὴ ἀπαντήσαι ἐμοὶ τοῦ καταλιπεῖν σε

translator_notes.note: Ruth’s declaration is a voluntary covenant commitment from a foreigner—a Moabite, a people Israel’s law explicitly excluded. That Ruth becomes the great-grandmother of David and an ancestor of Jesus makes this the biblical tradition’s strongest statement against ethnic exclusivism.
